newsSince: No material news relevant to the AI-node thesis since June 9. A U.S. tariff cut on agricultural equipment (25%→15%) is a mild macro positive for cost structure but unrelated to the rights-of-way/physical-build-out pathway, and Q1 2026 showed Industrial Equipment net orders -11%. The +5.9% move is tariff/macro-driven.

sources.label: Simply Wall St — Alamo tariff cut

verdicts.rankRationale: On the strict latent-node screen, the ag-tariff cut helps margins but is not new evidence on the rights-of-way/physical-build-out pathway, and Industrial Equipment orders fell 11% in Q1. Nothing strengthens the latent AI-adjacency case; hold flat.
verdicts.bull: Vegetation/infrastructure-maintenance equipment could ride physical build-out adjacent to data-center/grid expansion; tariff relief aids margin.
verdicts.neutral: Very loose AI link; demand is governed by municipal/ag cycles, not compute build-out.
verdicts.bear: Industrial orders down 11%; management guides slowing 2026 industrial sales; thesis remains speculative.
